meta.setting: Cold camps along wagon roads, creek bars, timber edges, and abandoned mining cuts in Jefferson country
meta.rdr_clone_target: RDR-clone western with trail evidence, missing travelers, scavenger sign, and hard outdoor survival

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Rugged mining, logging, ranching, road travel, and settlement conditions around Whiskeytown.

research_grounding.title: California Department of Fish and Wildlife Black Bear
research_grounding.use: Bear presence and scavenger pressure in California wildlands.

generation_prompt.task: Generate the Cold Camps region page for the 1899 Jefferson State western packet. This JSON contains shape and guidance only.
page_shape: H3 local marker line in the register of Dead Ash, Bedroll Mark, and Scattered Tin
square image reference immediately after the marker line
opening blockquote built as three short generated lines: abandoned object, track or weather detail, plain table meaning
two compact paragraphs establishing cold camps as evidence sites, rests gone wrong, ambush remains, missing-person starts, and survival pressure
closing blockquote that gives a generated camp sign and practical table meaning
generation_guidance: Generate cold camps as practical trail evidence: ash gone gray, bedroll dents, coffee grounds, hoof marks, boot scuffs, broken harness, bear sign, and tools left where people do not usually leave them.
Give the table hooks around missing travelers, raided freight, illness, a hurried departure, a staged disappearance, or a camp that should still be warm but is not.
Keep details tactile and readable; the page should help a table infer time, direction, number of people, animals present, and whether violence happened.
Treat the outdoors as unforgiving: wet blankets, sour beans, smoke in wool, hard ground, cold fingers, and noise traveling farther than expected.
generation_prompt.hand_cut_note: The best cold camp clue should be ordinary enough to make everybody uneasy.
generation_prompt.table_spark: A cold-camp scene should ask who slept here, what woke them, what they left behind, and which tracks are missing.
must_preserve: Use 1899 western trail, camp, and tracking language only.
The generated page must include image path images/cold-camps.jpg.
The prompt must not contain final prose, final blockquotes, or finished field marks.
avoid: do not mention any prior packet, inherited taxonomy, or non-western source language
do not make the camp eerie through supernatural implication
do not include modern camping gear, nylon, flashlights, cans with readable labels, or plastic objects
self_check: The future markdown follows the exact heading sequence.
The future page is about trail evidence, survival, wildlife, and scene starts.
The prompt contains no finished page prose.
All language belongs to grounded 1899 Jefferson State western play.
